overview.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases, Shared DB or directory, Authentication issues

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-10189 [CWE-22]: Microsoft Exchange Server Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: The threat actors targeted specific employees in the energy sector with tailored phishing emails containing malicious attachments. They exploited a vulnerability in Microsoft Exchange Server (CVE-2020-10189) to gain initial access to the victim's system.

stage.name: Execution
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.kev: CVE-2025-5777 [CWE-918]: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
stage.description: After gaining initial access, the attackers uploaded a simple web shell onto the compromised system to maintain persistence and execute commands remotely. This was achieved by exploiting a vulnerability that allowed unrestricted upload of files with dangerous types (CVE-2025-5777).

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: The attackers defaced public-facing web servers and websites belonging to the energy organization, leaving behind a message claiming responsibility for the attack. This was done to cause embarrassment and disrupt the organization's operations.

overview.summary: The Unitronics Defacement Campaign targeted the energy sector using spear-phishing techniques to gain initial access, followed by web shell deployment for persistence, culminating in website defacement.
